advisoryBylaws.metaTitle: Advisory Council Bylaws — Mongolian Heritage Center
advisoryBylaws.metaDescription: Operating rules of the Advisory Council of the Mongolian Heritage Center, adopted by the Board of Directors on November 21, 2023.
advisoryBylaws.subtitle: Adopted by resolution of the Board of Directors of the “Mongolian Heritage Center” at its meeting on November 21, 2023.

sections.text: The “Mongolian Heritage Center” (hereinafter the “Center”) is a Non-For-Profit Corporation that conducts ongoing activities in the public interest, serving the cultural needs of people of Mongolian descent residing in the central-western region of the United States; passing Mongolian language, heritage, history, and traditions on to future generations; teaching them; and strengthening the Mongolian spirit.

articles.title: Structure, headcount, and organization of the Advisory Council

sections.text: The Advisory Council may have up to 11 members.
Members of the Advisory Council are selected for a 3-year term by resolution of the Board of Directors on the proposal of the Board Chair.
At the member's own request, an Advisory Council member may continue to serve for an additional 3-year term.
The Advisory Council shall meet at least once a year.
A joint meeting of the Center's Board of Directors and the Advisory Council shall be organized at least once a year.

articles.title: Rights and duties of Advisory Council members

sections.text: Advisory Council members shall be guided by the Center's bylaws and other rules and procedures adopted in conformity with them.
Members shall provide methodological advice and support for the implementation of the Center's purposes and areas of activity, evaluate activities, and provide recommendations.
Members have the right to bring proposals, recommendations, errors, and shortcomings related to the Center's activities directly to the Board Chair.
The Advisory Council does not participate in the day-to-day activities of the Center and does not represent the Center.

articles.title: Rights and duties of the Center

sections.text: The Center shall endeavor to incorporate and implement in its activities the proposals and recommendations made by the Advisory Council and its members.

The Center's Manager may communicate directly with Advisory Council members regarding day-to-day operations and any ceremonial or commemorative events, and may seek advice, recommendations, and support.
Neither the Advisory Council members nor the Center shall bear any liability in connection with the exercise of the rights and duties set forth in these bylaws.

advisoryBylaws.viewMainBylawsLead: See the full main bylaws of the Center.

bylaws.metaDescription: Full bylaws of the Mongolian Heritage Center, adopted by the Board of Directors at its meeting on January 18, 2026.
bylaws.subtitle: Reviewed and re-adopted by resolution of the Board of Directors at its meeting on January 18, 2026.

intro.title: General provisions
paragraphs: The “Mongolian Heritage Center” is a Non-For-Profit Corporation that conducts ongoing activities in the public interest, serving the cultural needs of people of Mongolian descent residing in the central-western region of the United States; passing Mongolian language, heritage, history, and traditions on to future generations; teaching them; and strengthening the Mongolian spirit.
The “Mongolian Heritage Center” is located in and around Chicago. (Hereinafter the “Center”.)
Name in English: “Mongolian Heritage Center” (abbreviated as “MHC”).

sections.text: Create welcoming conditions for people of Mongolian descent to meet regularly, build friendships, learn from one another, and exchange information;
Teach and pass on to the next generation Mongolian language, heritage, arts, literature, and traditions;
Deepen and develop the friendship and mutual understanding between the Mongolian and American peoples.
Mongolian language and script instruction;
Instruction in Mongolian literature and history;
Mongolian library;
Lectures, seminars, and conferences;
Instruction in traditional Mongolian arts, song, music, dance, and classical music, and the organization of performances and concerts;
Instruction and tournaments in national and other sports and traditional board games;
Instruction in traditional Mongolian crafts, dress, and cuisine;
Religious observances, gatherings, and meditation as permitted under applicable local law;
Organization of national holidays, ceremonies, official events, and receptions;
Exhibitions and marketplaces featuring traditional Mongolian goods;
Programs for families, youth, and elders;
Charitable activities for people of Mongolian descent.

articles.title: Rights and duties of the Center

sections.text: The Center shall conduct its activities in compliance with the laws and regulations of the United States and the State of Illinois governing nonprofit organizations, and with bylaws and rules adopted thereunder.

The Center shall support and coordinate the implementation of proposals and initiatives that align with the purpose stated in its bylaws, providing administrative, operational, and other support, and shall cooperate with such efforts.
The Center may enter into mutually beneficial cooperation agreements with relevant nonprofit organizations and individuals in the United States and other countries.
The Center has the right to own real and movable property, to register its ownership, and to use and dispose of it.
The Center shall have a membership. Membership dues shall be set by the Board of Directors. The Center may also have honorary members and members of any nationality.
The Center may issue its own named awards and certificates of merit.
The Center shall have its own logo and seal, and shall use printed letterhead.
The Center shall maintain its own website, Facebook group, email address, mailing address, telephone, and fax, and may maintain its own publication.
The Center shall maintain operating and donation accounts.
The Center's financial activities shall be conducted on transparent and open principles, and the Center shall have the right to conduct any donation activity.

articles.title: Structure, the supreme governing body, and its authorities

sections.text: The supreme governing body of the Center shall be the Board of Directors. The Board shall meet at least twice a year (mid-year and year-end), discuss the Center's activity and financial reports, deliberate on and set any donations, taxes, fees, salaries, incentives, and discounts, and determine the policy and direction of future activities.
The Chair of the Board of Directors shall be the Consul of Mongolia residing in Chicago, USA.

The Board of Directors shall consist of no fewer than 5 and no more than 11 members. A Board member shall be a person of Mongolian descent who meets the established requirements. Members shall be selected by the Board through a public, open call. The ethical and other requirements for Board members shall be set by the Board.
A Board member's term shall be 2 years; performance shall be reviewed by the Board, and the term may be extended once more for an additional 2 years.
Board members need not be appointed simultaneously. The Board may have an attached Secretary, Advisory Council, Audit Committee, and Finance & Accounting Unit.

If a Board member commits an ethical violation during their term, fails to perform their duties, or for specific reasons becomes unable to perform their duties and submits a written request, the matter shall be resolved at a regular or special meeting of the Board.

sections.heading: The Board of Directors shall exercise the following authorities and duties:

sections.text: Adopt and amend the bylaws and internal rules of the Center.
Discuss, approve, and oversee the Center's annual activity and financial reports and the draft budget and budget.
Develop and approve activity programs, plans, and projects, and oversee their implementation.
Appoint and remove the Center's Executive Director on the Chair's proposal.
The Center's Manager and members of the management team shall be appointed and removed by the Executive Director in consultation with the Board Chair.
Approve the structure and headcount of the Center, approve the budget, and set salaries and incentives.
Approve the types, amounts, and applicable discounts of the Center's donations, taxes, and fees.
Deliberate on and decide the dissolution of the Center.
The quorum for a Board meeting shall be at least two-thirds of the members.
If consensus is not reached, decisions shall be made by simple majority of the members present at the vote.
At the request of two-thirds of Board members, a special meeting of the Board may be called. The request shall be submitted to the Chair in writing.
Board meetings shall produce minutes and resolutions, the execution of which the Board shall oversee.
Board meetings may be held electronically and votes may be collected electronically; minutes and resolutions from electronic meetings shall have the same force as those from ordinary meetings.

sections.heading: The Board Chair shall have the following authorities and duties:

sections.text: Determine the policy and direction of the Center and lead its activities within the bylaws.
Schedule and convene regular and special Board meetings, and issue resolutions and directives in accordance with their decisions.
Submit the Center's Executive Director to the Board for approval, and remove.
Enter into a contract with the Executive Director and oversee its execution.
Appoint and remove an interim Acting Director in the Executive Director's absence.
May assign specific duties and tasks to Board members in furtherance of the Center's activity areas and objectives.
The Chair shall cast a tie-breaking vote in the event of a tied vote.

sections.heading: A Board member shall have the following authorities and duties:

sections.text: Participate in scheduled Board meetings with proposals and initiatives, each holding a single vote.
A Board member's vote shall be independent and impartial.
Have unimpeded access to the Center's activity reports and financial information.
May take responsibility for a specific area assigned by the Chair.
A Board member who has made a valuable contribution to strengthening and developing the Center's activities may receive appropriate recognition.
A Board member shall pay the annual member dues set by the Board at the start of each financial year.

Board members shall attend meetings except for valid reasons. Missing two consecutive meetings without valid reason constitutes an ethical violation, and the Board may propose and resolve at a meeting to remove the member.

sections.heading: The Executive Director shall have the following authorities and duties:

sections.text: Implement the policies and decisions of the Board of Directors;
Prepare the Center's annual plan, budget, programs, and event programs; obtain Board approval; and implement and report on them;
Organize the Center's finances, administration, and the activities listed in the areas of activity in compliance with U.S. federal and state laws, the Center's mission, and these bylaws;
Represent the Center to Mongolia and other countries, and to the public, partners, and donors;
Select the Manager and members of the management team, present them to the Board Chair, and have them appointed or removed by the Board;
Enter into negotiations and contracts on behalf of the Center within the direction, approved plans, and programs given by the Board.
Oversee the day-to-day activities of the Center and report to the Board from time to time on plan performance, project and program outcomes, finances, and risks;
The Executive Director shall attend Board meetings.
The Center shall have the real property, assets, and sources of funding necessary to carry out the purposes and directions stated in its bylaws.
The ultimate owner of the Center's real property shall be the “Mongolian Heritage Center.” No individual or legal entity shall hold an ownership share or have the right to dispose of it.

sections.heading: The Center shall fund the purchase of real property and the activities listed in its areas of activity from the following sources:

sections.text: Donations and contributions from individuals and legal entities;
Membership dues;
Income from activities related to carrying out the purposes of the bylaws;
Annual dues from Board members. The amount of annual dues shall be set by the Board at a meeting and paid once a year.
Other.

sections.heading: The Center shall be dissolved under the following conditions:

sections.text: Two-thirds of the Board members have decided that there is no need to continue the activities stated in the Center's bylaws;
A decision has been issued by local law or judicial authorities for compulsory dissolution of the Center.

Upon dissolution, the Center's real property shall be transferred to a successor organization with similar purposes that conducts activities in the interests of the Mongolian community in the central U.S. region.
Other property shall be transferred to such successor organization without dispute; if no such organization exists, it shall be resolved in accordance with applicable local law.
These bylaws shall be adopted at the inaugural meeting of the Board of Directors and shall remain in effect indefinitely from the date of adoption.
These bylaws shall be issued only in Mongolian and English. In the event of any conflict in content between the Mongolian and English versions, the Mongolian original shall govern.

bylaws.viewGovernanceLead: See the governance structure and RACI matrix based on Article 4 of these bylaws.

footer.tagline: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization based in Chicago, USA, dedicated to preserving and passing on Mongolian language, heritage, and traditions to future generations.
footer.addressLine: 1301 S Wolf Road, Prospect Heights, Illinois 60070, USA
footer.copyright: © {year} Mongolian Heritage Center. All rights reserved.

testimonials.title: Voices of our donors and supporters

items.name: N. Jantsannorov
items.role: Twice State Prize laureate of Mongolia, People's Artist, Hero of Labor, composer
items.quote: Contributing to the work of bringing together our Mongolian brothers and sisters living abroad, and preserving and passing on our heritage to future generations, is a particularly meaningful honor for me. This Center, founded in the Mongolian community around Chicago, will nourish the next generation not only through words and music but also through thought and upbringing.

items.name: B. Altantuya
items.quote: When I brought my children, seeing Mongolian language, history, and heritage come alive in front of them made a deep impression. The teachers here are knowledgeable and have a real gift for working with children. We parents could feel firsthand how our children's knowledge of Mongolian and their sense of homeland grew through these programs.

items.name: J. Erdenebat
items.quote: If you want a deep understanding of Mongolian history and heritage, this is a must-visit place. The collection and rare archival materials have been an invaluable contribution to my research. The open discussions and lectures for Mongolian scholars abroad are bringing new life to the intellectual side of our community.

faq.metaTitle: Frequently Asked Questions — Mongolian Heritage Center
faq.metaDescription: Common questions about the Mongolian Heritage Center, donations, events, room rentals, and membership.
faq.lead: Answers to the most common questions about the Mongolian Heritage Center, donations, events, and room rentals. If you can't find your answer here, please reach out directly.
faq.noResults: No questions match your search. Try a different keyword.

items.q: What is the Mongolian Heritage Center?
items.a: The Mongolian Heritage Center is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization based in Illinois, USA. Our mission is to preserve the Mongolian language, heritage, and traditions for future generations in the U.S., and to unite the Mongolian diaspora community.

items.q: When and where was it founded?
items.a: The Center was officially established in August 2023 in Chicago (Prospect Heights, IL — 1301 S Wolf Road, Prospect Heights, IL 60070).

items.q: What is your IRS tax ID (EIN)?
items.a: Our IRS EIN is 93-3162554. Donations to the Center are tax-deductible in accordance with U.S. federal law.

items.q: Who runs the Center?
items.a: The Center is governed by an 11-member Board of Directors, an Executive management team, and a 6-member Advisory Council. You can meet the team on the Team page.

items.q: Is the Center a nonprofit?
items.a: Yes. We are an IRS 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit organization. Our bylaws, financials, and leadership reports are transparent and publicly available.

items.q: How can I donate?
items.a: Visit the Donate page and choose Zeffy, Stripe, or PayPal. You can also donate by bank transfer or check — contact info@mongolianheritagecenter.org for instructions.

items.q: Are donations tax-deductible?
items.a: Yes. As an IRS 501(c)(3) organization, donations from U.S. taxpayers are fully tax-deductible. You'll receive an official receipt after donating.

items.q: What payment methods do you accept?
items.a: Credit and debit cards (Visa, MasterCard, Amex, Discover), ACH bank transfers, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and PayPal balance. Zeffy is our recommended option because it charges 0% platform fees.

items.q: Can I donate anonymously?
items.a: Yes. When donating, select the “List me as anonymous” option. Your name will not appear on the donor list or public acknowledgments.

items.q: Can I donate goods or services (in-kind)?
items.a: Yes. We accept in-kind donations of books, equipment, and materials, as well as volunteer professional services. Please coordinate in advance by emailing info@mongolianheritagecenter.org.

items.q: How do I get a tax receipt?
items.a: You'll automatically receive an email receipt after your online donation. If you don't see it, check your spam folder — otherwise reach out to info@mongolianheritagecenter.org and we'll resend it.

items.q: What are the donor tiers?
items.a: We recognize donors across 7 tiers based on cumulative giving: Spark (Оч), Fire (Гал), Star (Од), Moon (Сар), Sun (Нар), Sky (Тэнгэр), and Diamond (Очир). See the Donors page for details.

items.q: How can I attend an event?
items.a: Upcoming events are announced on the home page and via our email newsletter. Some events require pre-registration; others are open to walk-ins. Registering in advance is always the safest option.

items.q: How can I volunteer?
items.a: Fill out the Contact form and select “Volunteer” as the reason. A team coordinator will reach out with roles that match your interests.

items.q: Do I need to be Mongolian to participate?
items.a: Not at all! Our events are open to everyone — friends, families, and anyone interested in learning about Mongolian language and culture is warmly welcomed.

items.q: Do you have programs for children?
items.a: Yes. We regularly organize Mongolian-language classes, cultural activities, and community events for children and youth. Details are posted on the home page and social channels.

items.q: Do I need a paid membership to participate?
items.a: No formal paid membership is required. However, we recommend subscribing to our newsletter so you're the first to hear about new programs and events.

items.q: How do I rent a room?
items.a: Browse the Rentals page for room rates and types, then send a booking request via the Contact form or by emailing info@mongolianheritagecenter.org. Bookings are handled first-come, first-served.

items.q: Do donors get a discount?
items.a: Yes. Donor rates apply to individuals and organizations who contribute — for example, Hall 208 is $800/day at the donor rate vs. $1,200 at the base rate. See the Rentals page for the full table.

items.q: Is the tea room free?
items.a: Yes. The tea room is free to use for both donors and standard users, at any hourly, daily, or monthly rate.

items.q: Can I pay by check or bank transfer?
items.a: Yes. Bank transfer details and invoice terms will be sent once your booking is confirmed.

items.q: Can I cancel my booking?
items.a: Cancellations made at least 7 days before the event date are eligible for a deposit refund. After that, cancellations are handled case-by-case per our bylaws.

items.q: How does my organization start a partnership?
items.a: Send us a short (2–3 page) proposal and we'll set up a meeting with our partnerships team. Start by emailing info@mongolianheritagecenter.org.

items.q: Can I sponsor the Center?
items.a: Absolutely. We accept event, program, and scholarship sponsorships. We can send a sponsorship pack outlining tiers and impact — just ask.

items.q: Can I join as an advisor?
items.a: Advisors are appointed by the Board of Directors based on qualifications and mission fit. To express interest, contact us at info@mongolianheritagecenter.org after reviewing the current Team page.

items.q: Where can I find financial reports?
items.a: Annual activity and financial reports are made public. You can find them on the About section of the website, or request them directly at info@mongolianheritagecenter.org.

faq.ctaLead: Send us your question directly — we typically reply within 3 business days.

terms.metaTitle: Terms of Service — Mongolian Heritage Center
terms.metaDescription: Terms of service for using the Mongolian Heritage Center website and services.
terms.subtitle: Last updated: July 4, 2026

intro.title: Overview
paragraphs: These Terms of Service (“Terms”) govern the relationship between the Mongolian Heritage Center — a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, IRS EIN: 93-3162554 — and users of its “mongolianheritagecenter.org” website, services, and events.
By accessing this website or participating in the Center's activities, you agree to these Terms in full. If you do not agree, please do not use the website.

sections.text: The Center is a nonprofit (501(c)(3)) organization dedicated to preserving Mongolian language, heritage, and traditions, and uniting the Mongolian community.
The website and other digital channels exist to communicate the Center's mission, share information about events, donations, and membership, and engage the public.
Donations to the Center are tax-deductible in accordance with U.S. federal law. The IRS tax-exempt EIN is 93-3162554.
Donations are voluntary and generally non-refundable. If made in error, contact the Center within 30 days for resolution.
Donation records are kept transparent and reported in annual financials. Donors may request to remain anonymous, in which case the name will not be displayed.
You may register for and attend events, classes, and gatherings organized by the Center. Some events may require a registration fee.
Participants must follow organizer instructions and safety rules. The Center may revoke participation rights for ethical violations or misconduct that affects other participants.
The Center may use event photography and recordings for its digital channels and reports. If you do not wish to appear, please notify the organizer before the event.
All text, images, logos, and design on this website are the intellectual property of the Center or its authorized licensors.
Users may not reproduce, redistribute, or use the content for commercial purposes without prior written consent from the Center.
Works contributed by artists and creators are used by the Center with proper attribution to the original author.
Using the website for illegal, fraudulent, or infringing purposes.
Harassing, defaming, or discriminating against other users or visitors.
Compromising the website's security or issuing high volumes of automated requests (bots, scrapers).
The Center provides the website and services on an “as is” basis. The Center makes no warranty of continuous, error-free operation.
The Center is not responsible for the content or security of third-party websites linked from this site.
To the fullest extent permitted by law, the Center shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.
The Center may modify these Terms at any time. Updates will be marked with a new date on this page.
Continued use of the website after updates constitutes acceptance of the new Terms.
These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Illinois, USA.
For questions, feedback, or complaints, contact info@mongolianheritagecenter.org or write to 1301 S Wolf Road, Prospect Heights, IL 60070.

terms.viewPrivacyLead: See how the Center processes and protects your personal information.

privacy.metaDescription: How the Mongolian Heritage Center collects, uses, and protects your personal information.
privacy.subtitle: Last updated: July 4, 2026

intro.title: Overview
paragraphs: The Mongolian Heritage Center is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains what information we collect, how we use it, and how we safeguard it.
By using the website or the Center's services, you agree to the terms of this policy.

sections.text: Name, email, phone, and mailing address — collected during donations, membership signup, or event registration.
Payment information — processed through third-party providers such as Zeffy, Stripe, and PayPal. We do not store credit card numbers.
Feedback and messages — text submitted through our “Contact” form.
IP address, browser type, pages visited, and visit timestamps — through standard server logs.
Cookies and similar technologies (see Section Three).
Process and confirm donations, memberships, and event registrations.
Send tax-deductible donation receipts.
Send newsletters and event invitations (you may opt out at any time).
Analyze website performance and improve services.
Comply with legal obligations, such as 501(c)(3) reporting.
We may use cookies for functional purposes (such as remembering your language preference) and analytics purposes (such as counting visits).
You can disable cookies through your browser settings, though this may affect some site functionality.
We share your information only with Center staff and authorized volunteers who need it to serve you.
We share information with third-party service providers only as needed to operate our services. These include:
Payment processors (Zeffy, Stripe, PayPal) — each has its own privacy policy.
Email delivery services (e.g., mailing list providers).
We do not sell or rent your information to third parties for commercial purposes.
We may disclose information in response to lawful legal requests.
We retain information for as long as needed to fulfill the purpose it was collected for. Donation and reporting data are retained for the number of years required by U.S. tax law.
We use standard security measures including HTTPS encryption and access controls. However, no method of transmission over the Internet is 100% secure.
You have the right to request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal information.
To unsubscribe from newsletters, use the “Unsubscribe” link in the message or email info@mongolianheritagecenter.org.
For data-processing questions, contact info@mongolianheritagecenter.org.
Our website is not directed to children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect information from them.
If we inadvertently collect information from a child under 13, parents or guardians may contact us to have it removed.
We may update this policy periodically. Updates will be marked with a new date on this page.
Significant changes will be announced by email or on the website's homepage.
For questions, feedback, or complaints:
Email: info@mongolianheritagecenter.org
Mailing address: 1301 S Wolf Road, Prospect Heights, IL 60070, USA

events.upcomingLead: Mark your calendar — pre-registration is recommended for events marked RSVP.

items.title: Naadam Festival Celebration
items.description: Celebrate the "Three Manly Games" — wrestling, archery, and horsemanship — with music, food, and family activities marking Mongolia's national holiday.

items.title: Mongolian Language Class — Beginner
items.description: A weekly introductory class covering the Cyrillic alphabet, greetings, and everyday conversation for children and adults.

items.title: Community Potluck & Cultural Night
items.description: Bring a dish to share and enjoy an evening of traditional music, storytelling, and community connection.

items.title: Annual Fundraising Gala
items.description: An evening of dinner, live performances, and a silent auction supporting the Center's programs and community services.

items.title: Tsagaan Sar (Lunar New Year) Gathering
items.description: Families gathered to celebrate the Lunar New Year with traditional greetings, buuz, and festive dress.

items.title: Spring Language Class Showcase
items.description: Students from our weekly language classes shared songs, poems, and short conversations with family and friends.

home.metaTitle: Mongolian Heritage Center — Preserving heritage, building community
home.metaDescription: A 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization based in Chicago, USA. We preserve Mongolian language, heritage, and traditions, and pass them on to future generations.
home.h1Pre: Let's pass our Mongolian language and heritage
home.lead: The Mongolian Heritage Center is a nonprofit organization based in Chicago, USA, dedicated to bringing together people of Mongolian descent, passing on our language and culture to the next generation, and strengthening the friendship between the Mongolian and American peoples.
home.goalsLead: Our work is guided by three core goals.

goals.title: Bringing the community together
goals.description: Creating welcoming spaces and opportunities for people of Mongolian descent to meet, build friendships, learn from one another, and share information.

goals.title: Passing heritage to the next generation
goals.description: Teaching the next generation our Mongolian language, heritage, arts, literature, and traditions, and passing them on.

goals.title: Strengthening friendship
goals.description: Deepening and expanding the friendship and mutual understanding between the Mongolian and American peoples.

home.focusLead: From language and literature to arts, sports, holidays, and charitable giving — a wide range of activities to preserve and pass on our heritage.

focusAreas.title: Literature & history
focusAreas.description: Instruction in Mongolian literature and history.

focusAreas.title: Mongolian library
focusAreas.description: Library services and a curated Mongolian collection.

focusAreas.title: Arts, song & music
focusAreas.description: Instruction in traditional Mongolian arts, song, music, dance, and classical music — plus concerts and performances.

focusAreas.title: Sports & board games
focusAreas.description: Instruction and tournaments in national and other sports, as well as traditional board games.

focusAreas.title: Crafts, dress & cuisine
focusAreas.description: Instruction in traditional Mongolian crafts, dress, and cuisine.

focusAreas.title: Religious observance
focusAreas.description: Religious observances, gatherings, and meditation permitted under local law.

focusAreas.title: Holidays & ceremonies
focusAreas.description: National holidays, ceremonies, official events, and receptions.

focusAreas.title: Exhibitions & marketplaces
focusAreas.description: Exhibitions and marketplaces featuring traditional Mongolian goods.

focusAreas.title: Family, youth & elders
focusAreas.description: Programs for families, youth, and elders.

focusAreas.title: Charity & giving
focusAreas.description: Charitable activities for people of Mongolian descent.

home.communityLead: The number of Mongolians living in the central United States has grown year after year and is now the largest Mongolian community in the U.S. To serve this expanding community's cultural and spiritual needs, a permanent center is essential.
communityPoints: Bringing people of Mongolian descent together
Passing language and heritage to the next generation
home.buildingP1: For the Center to operate continuously, securing a permanent home is essential. With our own space, every member of the Mongolian community and organizations dedicated to the well-being of our people will have a place to operate consistently.
home.buildingP2: It will also open new opportunities for Mongolian citizens and businesses seeking to enter the U.S. market.
home.donateTitle: Your donation helps preserve our heritage for future generations
home.donateLead: The Mongolian Heritage Center is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, and donations are tax-deductible.

about.metaDescription: The Mongolian Heritage Center is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization founded in Chicago, USA, in 2023. We are dedicated to passing on Mongolian language, heritage, and traditions to the next generation.
about.lead: The Mongolian Heritage Center is a nonprofit organization founded in August 2023 in Chicago, USA, dedicated to passing Mongolian language, heritage, and traditions on to future generations.
about.introP1: The Mongolian Heritage Center serves people of Mongolian descent living abroad — especially the next generation — by introducing them to and passing on their native language, history, heritage, and traditions.
about.introP2: On one hand, we bring Mongolians together; on the other, we work to strengthen the friendship and mutual understanding between the Mongolian and American peoples.
about.missionText: To meet the cultural needs of people of Mongolian descent living in the central and midwestern United States; to preserve knowledge of Mongolian language, heritage, and traditions; to pass them on to future generations; and to foster understanding of Mongolian heritage among our friends abroad.
about.visionText: To cherish and preserve our distinctive Mongolian heritage in a globalized world, and to pass it on for future generations to know and appreciate.
about.goalsLead: Our work is guided by three core goals.
about.greetingSalutation: Dear Mongolian brothers and sisters living in the central United States,
greetingParagraphs: The number of Mongolians living in the central United States has steadily grown in recent years, and it is now the largest Mongolian community in the U.S. The need to establish a permanent cultural center in the Chicago area to serve the cultural and spiritual needs of this expanding community has become clear.
And so, residents in and around Chicago founded the “Mongolian Heritage Center” nonprofit organization, registering it under applicable law and beginning operations.

Securing a permanent home is essential for the Center's ongoing work. With our own space, every member of the Mongolian community and organizations devoted to our people's well-being will have a place to operate — and Mongolian citizens and businesses seeking to enter the U.S. market will also find new opportunities.
We invite you to make a real contribution to bringing the central-U.S. Mongolian community's development to a new stage and to promoting our heritage. May all good deeds prosper.
about.structureLead: Our governance structure from the Board of Directors down to the Center's staff.
about.structureCta: View duties and the RACI matrix
about.donateTitle: Your donation helps preserve our heritage for future generations
about.donateText: The Mongolian Heritage Center is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, and donations are tax-deductible. We are committed to keeping the use of donations transparent and open.
about.ctaLead: Learn more about our work and upcoming events on our website and Facebook page.

contact.metaDescription: Get in touch with the Mongolian Heritage Center: 1301 S Wolf Road, Prospect Heights, Illinois 60070. Phone +1 (872) 231-0808, info@mongolianheritagecenter.org.
contact.lead: Have a question about our programs, events, partnerships, or donations? Send us a message — we'd be glad to hear from you.
contact.infoLead: You can reach us directly through any of the channels below.
contact.formLead: Fill out the form and we'll get back to you.

form.messagePlaceholder: Tell us what's on your mind...
form.successText: Thank you for reaching out. We'll review your message and get back to you within 1–2 business days.

contact.mapAddress: 1301 S Wolf Road, Prospect Heights, Illinois 60070, USA

donate.metaDescription: Make a tax-deductible donation to the Mongolian Heritage Center. Give securely via Stripe, PayPal, or Zeffy. IRS EIN: 93-3162554.
donate.lead: The Mongolian Heritage Center is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, and donations are tax-deductible. Your gift funds language classes, cultural events, and the day-to-day work of building a permanent home for our community.

impactItems.title: Language & cultural classes
impactItems.description: Mongolian language, script, history, and traditions taught to the next generation.

impactItems.title: Cultural events
impactItems.description: National holidays, concerts, exhibitions, lectures, and seminars.

impactItems.title: A permanent home
impactItems.description: Rent, build-out, and operations for a permanent community center.

donate.amountsLead: Every gift makes a difference. These are just suggestions — give what feels right.
donate.providersTitle: Choose how you'd like to give
donate.providersLead: Give securely online through any of the trusted services below.

zeffy.title: Zeffy
zeffy.description: Zero processing fees, so 100% of your gift goes to the Center. Built specifically for 501(c)(3) organizations.

stripe.title: Stripe
stripe.description: Industry-leading secure payments. Accepts all major credit and debit cards.

paypal.title: PayPal
paypal.description: Donate using your PayPal account or directly with a card.

zelle.title: Zelle
zelle.description: Send a direct, fee-free transfer from your US bank's Zelle app. Enter our email or phone as the recipient.

donate.otherLead: You can also mail a check or contribute through your employer.
donate.checkPayTo: Make checks payable to “Mongolian Heritage Center.”
donate.matchingText: Many employers match charitable gifts from their employees. Ask your HR department whether your company has a matching program.
donate.taxNote: All donations are tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law. IRS EIN: 93-3162554. You will receive an emailed acknowledgment for your records.

donateRegister.metaTitle: Register a donation — Mongolian Heritage Center
donateRegister.metaDescription: Register your monetary, in-kind, or service donation with the Mongolian Heritage Center. Verified donations are published on our donor roll.
donateRegister.lead: Beyond monetary gifts, you can also register in-kind and service donations here. Once we verify the details, we add your name to the donor roll.

steps.title: Make your donation
steps.description: Give through Zeffy, Stripe, PayPal, Zelle, or by check — in-kind and service donations count too.

steps.title: Fill out the form
steps.description: Submit your donation details using the form below. It only takes 2 minutes.

steps.title: We verify it
steps.description: Within 3–5 business days we match and verify the details, then add you to the donor roll.

donateRegister.formLead: Fields marked with an asterisk (*) are required.

form.notePlaceholder: Anything else you'd like us to know...
form.publicLabel: You may publish my name on the donor roll
form.publicHint: If unchecked, your gift is counted among anonymous donations.
form.errorText: Something went wrong while sending. Please try again in a moment, or email us at info@mongolianheritagecenter.org.
form.successText: Thank you! Once we verify your donation details, we will add your name to the donor roll.

donateRegister.helpText: If you have any questions about donation registration — or your name is misspelled on the roll — feel free to contact us.

governance.metaDescription: Governance structure, allocation of authorities and responsibilities, and the RACI matrix of the Mongolian Heritage Center. Based on bylaws Chapter 4, adopted by the Board on January 18, 2026.
governance.lead: Our governance structure, the allocation of authorities and responsibilities, and the RACI matrix. Based on the bylaws adopted at the Board meeting of January 18, 2026. Click any node to see detailed authorities and duties.
governance.orgLead: Click any node to see the detailed authorities and duties from the bylaws.
governance.asideLead: Supporting bodies that may be attached to the Board (Art. 4.5).
governance.raciLead: A matrix identifying who is accountable, responsible, consulted, and informed for each activity. Click a column header to highlight that role.
governance.raciNote: Note: each row has exactly one A (Accountable). The Executive Director's term length and KPIs are not specified in the bylaws, so clarifying them by contract reduces governance risk.
governance.ctaLead: See our mission, vision, and the Chair's message.

board.title: Board of Directors
board.subtitle: 5–11 members · 2-year term
duties: Adopt and amend the bylaws and internal rules (4.7.1).
Approve the annual activity report, financial report, and budget, and oversee them (4.7.2).
Approve programs, plans, and projects, and oversee their implementation (4.7.3).
Appoint and remove the Executive Director upon the Chair's proposal (4.7.4).
Set the organizational structure, headcount, compensation, donations, and fees (4.7.5, 4.7.6).
Decide on the dissolution of the Center (4.7.7).
Quorum is two-thirds of members; decisions by simple majority (4.8, 4.9).

chair.title: Board Chair
chair.subtitle: Consul in Chicago
duties: Set the Center's policy and direction and lead within the bylaws (4.13.1).
Call Board meetings and issue resolutions/directives (4.13.2).
Propose the Executive Director to the Board for approval, and remove (4.13.3).
Sign the contract with the Executive Director and oversee its execution (4.13.4).
Appoint an interim Acting Director in the Director's absence (4.13.5).
Cast a tie-breaking vote (4.13.7).

secretary.title: Secretary
duties: A unit attached to the Board (4.5).
Handles meeting logistics, minutes, and records management.
Specific duties are set by the Board / Chair.

advisory.title: Advisory Council
duties: Serves in an advisory capacity attached to the Board (4.5).
Provides expert guidance on policy and programs.

control.title: Audit Committee
duties: Serves an oversight function attached to the Board (4.5).
Provides independent oversight of operational and financial performance.

finance.title: Finance & Accounting Unit
duties: Maintains finance and accounting records attached to the Board (4.5).
Manages operating and donation accounts (3.9).
Ensures financial transparency and openness (3.10).

director.title: Executive Director
director.subtitle: Reports to the Board
duties: Implements Board policy and decisions (4.15.1).
Prepares the annual plan, budget, and programs and submits them to the Board for approval (4.15.2).
Organizes finance, administration, and operations in compliance with law and bylaws (4.15.3).
Represents the Center to the public, partners, and donors (4.15.4).
Selects and proposes the Manager and management team for appointment (4.15.5).
Enters contracts and agreements within approved limits (4.15.6).
Provides day-to-day oversight; reports performance, risk, and finances to the Board (4.15.7).

manager.title: Manager
manager.subtitle: Day-to-day operations
duties: Runs day-to-day operations and reports to the Executive Director (4.16.2).
Ensures the integrity of records, finances, and assets (4.16.3).
Oversees use of the facility and service / maintenance contracts (4.16.4).
Participates in strategic planning and budget preparation (4.16.5).
Leads the management team and volunteers (4.16.6).
Manages programs and projects; enforces internal policies (4.16.7, 4.16.8).

team.title: Management team
duties: Appointed by the Executive Director in consultation with the Chair (4.7.4.1).
Carries out operations under the Manager's direction (4.16.6).

volunteers.title: Volunteers
duties: Work under the direction and coordination of the Manager (4.16.6).
Support the Center's events and programs.

raciRows: Adopt or amend bylaws and internal rules
Approve the annual report and budget, oversee execution
Approve programs, plans, and projects, oversee them
Appoint and remove the Executive Director
Set structure, headcount, compensation, incentives
Set donation, tax, fee, and discount policies
Decision to dissolve the Center (2/3 vote)
Set policy and direction; provide leadership
Call Board meetings and issue resolutions
Contract with and oversee the Executive Director
Appoint an interim Acting Director
Implement Board policy and decisions
Prepare the annual plan, budget, and programs
Bring operations into compliance with law and bylaws
Represent the Center to the public, partners, donors
Select and propose the Manager and team for appointment
Enter contracts and agreements within approved limits
Report performance, risk, and finances to the Board
Run day-to-day operations and report
Maintain integrity of records, finances, assets
Oversee facility and service / maintenance contracts
Participate in strategy and budget preparation
Lead the team and volunteers
Manage programs and projects
Enforce internal rules and safety procedures
